title: raw_function name on RsaKey does not signal unsafe-primitive semantics
description: RsaKey::raw_function() performs a bare modular exponentiation with no padding. The name 'raw_function' (derived from the C wh_Client_RsaFunction name) does not communicate to contributors that this is a padding-free textbook RSA primitive. Callers who misread this as a PKCS1/PSS-safe sign/encrypt will produce cryptographically incorrect output with no compile-time or runtime indication. The doc comment does warn, but the function name itself is a maintainability hazard: future contributors adding PKCS1/OAEP wrappers may accidentally use raw_function as the implementation of a higher-level abstraction without adding padding. Consequence: silent cryptographic misuse that passes all current tests.
